block_01: IQ Option operates in different regions with different trading conditions. With this newsletter, we would like to remind you about the importance of keeping your traffic sources in compliance with the Affiliate Program rules of promotion, including
block_05: require specific rules to be followed, some require prior approval, and some are outright forbidden.
block_06: Let’s go over the main rules of promotion to avoid some common mistakes.
block_07: Disguising as the official channel of promotion.
block_08: While it is okay to use official logo and the brand name in your reviews, misleading the traders into believing your traffic source is the official one is not allowed. Websites, social media profiles or channels on video hosting platforms used as traffic sources should not contain the IQ Option brand name and should not copy the content of the official profiles or channels of the company.
block_10: The practice of bidding on IQ Option branded keywords is
block_12: . You can use search and display contextual advertising by using non-branded keywords and by promoting your own websites only.
block_13: Missing or not prominent risk warnings.
block_14: Do not forget to warn the traders about the risks associated with trading by placing the relevant risk warning near invitations to open an account (registration links). The risk warnings should not be concealed by their size, color, or in any other way.
block_15: Promoting the trading conditions not relevant to the target audience.
block_16: It is important to tailor your materials to the audience you plan to target. Depending on whether the material is intended for traders from
block_18: , you have to reflect the correct trading conditions available to them.
block_19: For example, do not promote binary options, higher leverages, trading tournaments, or VIP status to traders from the EEA countries. As for traders from non-EEA countries, it is important to not include any mention of regulation or CySEC in the materials targeting them. Using the materials with mixed information that targets both EEA and non-EEA audience is to be avoided.
block_20: Using outdated content and promo materials.
block_21: Most of the materials published a few years ago no longer reflect the current trading conditions and have to be updated. As for the promo materials, please make sure to use the up-to-date ones available in the
block_23: If you have any questions regarding your traffic sources, please let us know so that we can sort them out.
